name: Natural Park of Montes Obarenes - San Zadornil — circular from Frías
name_original: Parque Natural de Montes Obarenes - San Zadornil — circular desde Frías
region_intro: Touring cycling around Frías, Spain, offers diverse landscapes within the Burgos province of Castile and León. The region features rolling hills, gorges, and canyons, particularly around the Ebro River and within the Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil Natural Park. Terrain includes a mix of paved and unpaved sections, providing varied cycling experiences. The area is characterized by its significant landscape variety and historical landmarks. Best touring cycling routes around Frías The most popular touring cycling route is Natural Park of Montes Obarenes - San Zadornil — circular from Frías, a 20.6 miles (33.1 km) trail that takes about 2 hours 20 minutes to complete. This route offers an immersive experience through the natural park's varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Oña Historic Town Center – Trespaderne loop from Frías, a difficult 28.6 miles (46.0 km) path. This route explores historical towns and varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 55 minutes. Local touring cyclists also love the Frías Medieval Bridge – Hanging Houses of Frías loop from Frías, a 27.1 miles (43.6 km) trail leading through the iconic Frías Medieval Bridge and past the unique Hanging Houses of Frías, often completed in about 4 hours 25 minutes. Touring cycling around Frías is defined by the Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil Natural Park, the Ebro River canyons, and rolling hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, including challenging routes with significant elevation gains. The routes in Frías are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Frías's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Frías?
A: There are 14 touring cycling routes around Frías. These routes offer a variety of experiences, from moderate rides to more challenging paths, exploring the diverse landscapes of the Burgos province.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Frías?
A: The terrain around Frías is quite varied, featuring rolling hills, gorges, and canyons, particularly near the Ebro River and within the Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil Natural Park. You'll encounter a mix of paved and unpaved sections, providing an engaging experience for touring cyclists.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Frías?
A: Yes, several routes are circular. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030309012/natural-park-of-montes-obarenes-san-zadornil-circular-from-frias" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Park of Montes Obarenes - San Zadornil — circular from Frías&lt;/a&gt; offers an immersive experience through the natural park's varied landscapes. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31486179" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oña Historic Town Center – Trespaderne loop from Frías&lt;/a&gt;, which explores historical towns and varied terrain.

Q: What natural features can I see while cycling around Frías?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can cycle through the Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil Natural Park, known for its gorges, canyons, and woodlands. The Ebro River and its dramatic canyons are also prominent features. For specific natural attractions, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3079476" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Purón River Gorge&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4244321" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Humion peak&lt;/a&gt; for panoramic views.

Q: Are there any waterfalls accessible by touring bike near Frías?
A: Yes, the area around Frías features beautiful waterfalls. You can visit the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3929492" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tobera Waterfalls and Medieval Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1871481" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. There's also a dedicated &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4986755" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tobera Waterfalls Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; for stunning vistas.

Q: What historical landmarks can I explore on a touring bike in Frías?
A: Frías and its surroundings are steeped in history. You can cycle past the iconic Frías Medieval Bridge and the unique Hanging Houses of Frías, as seen on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31488174" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Frías Medieval Bridge – Hanging Houses of Frías loop from Frías&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable historical sites include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6365782" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poza de la Sal Salt Flats and Old Railway Station&lt;/a&gt;, offering a glimpse into the region's past.

Q: What is the difficulty level of touring cycling routes in Frías?
A: The routes around Frías cater to various skill levels, though many are considered challenging. Out of 14 routes, 10 are rated as difficult, and 4 as moderate. There are no easy routes listed, indicating that most paths involve significant elevation gains and varied terrain suitable for experienced touring cyclists.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Frías?
A: The touring cycling routes in Frías are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from more than 10 reviews. Over 200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the immersive experience within the natural park and the historical charm of the villages.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of Frías itself?
A: Yes, you can enjoy picturesque views of Frías on certain routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31487184" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medieval Bridge of Frías – View on Frías loop from Frías&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to offer stunning perspectives of the town and its medieval bridge. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40006664" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View on Frías – Trespaderne loop from Frías&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the longest touring cycling route available in Frías?
A: One of the longer routes is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31486179" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oña Historic Town Center – Trespaderne loop from Frías&lt;/a&gt;, which spans approximately 46.0 km (28.6 miles) and typically takes around 2 hours 55 minutes to complete, exploring historical towns and varied terrain.

Q: Can I find routes that combine natural parks with historical sites?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Frías offer a blend of natural beauty and historical significance. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030309012/natural-park-of-montes-obarenes-san-zadornil-circular-from-frias" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Park of Montes Obarenes - San Zadornil — circular from Frías&lt;/a&gt; takes you through the natural park, while also being close to the historic town of Frías itself. The region's character is defined by this unique combination.
tips: A very charming town. It maintains a medieval urban structure that crowns the castle of the Velasco family and the church of San Vicente. The houses are attached to each other, reminiscent of the hanging houses of Cuenca.
Views of the town of Frías with its castle that crowns the Cerro de la Muela and is one of the most spectacular rock castles in Castilla.

Bridge of Romanesque origin, from the 13th century, with 9 arches. One of the most valuable monuments of Frías. It was a means of communication, very important for trade between the Meseta and the Cantabrian coast.
